name: Pico Zapatero Circular Trail
name_original: Ruta del Pico Zapatero — circular desde Navandrinal
description: Hike the moderate 9.3-mile Pico Zapatero Circular Trail, gaining 2461 feet for panoramic views of the Sierra de Guadarrama and Gredos.
region_intro: Hiking around San Juan Del Molinillo offers diverse and rugged natural landscapes within the province of Ávila, Spain. The region is situated in the Alberche Valley, at the base of Pico Zapatero and on the southern slopes of the Sierra de la Paramera. This area features mountainous terrain, expansive pastures, and verdant meadows, providing varied environments for outdoor activities. The Sierra de la Paramera is part of the Central System mountain range, contributing to the area's significant elevation changes and panoramic views. Best hiking trails around San Juan Del Molinillo The most popular hiking route is Pico Zapatero Circular Trail, a 9.3 miles (14.9 km) moderate trail that takes 4 hours 53 minutes to complete. This route offers significant elevation gain and panoramic views from the summit of Pico Zapatero. Another top favourite among local hikers is Valle Navaluenga to the Embalse del Burguillo — circular by the Río Alberche, a moderate 8.6 miles (13.9 km) path. This trail follows the Río Alberche, offering views of the valley and the Embalse del Burguillo reservoir. Local hikers also love the La Cendra del Espino Trail (PRC-AV 12), a 9.8 miles (15.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 30 minutes. Hiking around San Juan Del Molinillo is defined by mountainous terrain, the Alberche Valley, and the Sierra de la Paramera, including the prominent Pico Zapatero. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate valley walks to more challenging mountain ascents. The routes in San Juan Del Molinillo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 200 hikers have used komoot to explore San Juan Del Molinillo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around San Juan Del Molinillo?
A: There are over 30 hiking trails in the San Juan Del Molinillo area, offering a range of experiences from easy walks to more challenging mountain ascents. You'll find 10 easy routes, 22 moderate routes, and 2 difficult routes to explore.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in San Juan Del Molinillo?
A: Yes, the region offers several circular routes. A notable one is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1138602625/pico-zapatero-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Zapatero Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 14.9 km path with significant elevation gain, offering panoramic views from the summit. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1145159848/valle-navaluenga-to-the-embalse-del-burguillo-circular-by-the-rio-alberche" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle Navaluenga to the Embalse del Burguillo — circular by the Río Alberche&lt;/a&gt;, an 8.6-mile route following the river.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on hikes in this area?
A: Hiking around San Juan Del Molinillo reveals a diverse landscape. You'll encounter mountainous terrain, expansive pastures, and verdant meadows within the Alberche Valley. As you ascend towards peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839745" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Zapatero&lt;/a&gt;, you might experience continuous waterfalls along the Arroyo Zapatero and traverse small, grassy meadows. The region is part of the Central System mountain range, offering dramatic scenery.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, for experienced trekkers, the area offers challenging routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1138536106/castillo-de-manqueospese-by-the-sierra-de-los-baldios-circular-from-villaviciosa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Manqueospese by the Sierra de los Baldíos — circular from Villaviciosa&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 27.8 km route with substantial elevation gain, providing a rewarding challenge.

Q: What are the best viewpoints or landmarks to see while hiking?
A: The region is rich in scenic viewpoints and landmarks. From the summit of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839745" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Zapatero&lt;/a&gt;, you can enjoy magnificent panoramic views encompassing Ávila city, the Valle de Amblés, the Sierra de Guadarrama, and the Central Massif of Gredos. Other notable points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5585611" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Navalmoral&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4144156" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portacho del Cuchillo&lt;/a&gt; mountain passes.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in San Juan Del Molinillo?
A: The hiking trails in San Juan Del Molinillo are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse and rugged natural landscapes, the panoramic views from higher elevations, and the variety of routes available for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any trails that follow a river or lead to a reservoir?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1145159848/valle-navaluenga-to-the-embalse-del-burguillo-circular-by-the-rio-alberche" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle Navaluenga to the Embalse del Burguillo — circular by the Río Alberche&lt;/a&gt; trail follows the Río Alberche, offering scenic views of the valley and leading to the Embalse del Burguillo reservoir.

Q: What is the typical duration for a hike in San Juan Del Molinillo?
A: Hike durations vary significantly depending on the route and your pace. Moderate trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1138726512/la-cendra-del-espino-trail-prc-av-12" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cendra del Espino Trail (PRC-AV 12)&lt;/a&gt; can take around 4 hours 30 minutes, while more challenging routes to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839745" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Zapatero&lt;/a&gt; can extend up to 7 hours for a full circular route.

Q: Are there any easy hiking options suitable for beginners?
A: Yes, San Juan Del Molinillo offers 10 easy routes, which are generally shorter and have less elevation gain, making them suitable for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed walk. These trails often explore the valley floor and gentler slopes.

Q: What is the highest point I can reach on a hike in this region?
A: The highest point accessible on a hike in the immediate vicinity is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839745" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Zapatero&lt;/a&gt;, which reaches an elevation of 2,158 to 2,160 meters. Hiking to its summit offers some of the most rewarding views in the area.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trails?
A: The trails in San Juan Del Molinillo feature varied terrain, including established paths, footpaths, and some cross-country sections, especially on higher mountain routes. You'll traverse mountainous areas, pastures, and fenced meadows, reflecting the diverse natural landscape of the Alberche Valley and Sierra de la Paramera.
tips: The unique granite landscape is the result of the mineral composition, climatic changes and the weathering of the rock surfaces, giving rise to suggestive modeling forms. The most striking process is the slabs, the boats, the corridors and alleys and the granular disintegration, creating an authentic labyrinth of stone sculptures. To the west, at the headwaters of the Adaja River, the white reliefs of La Serrota emerge. The path follows the ridge and collides with the top pyramid of Zapatero in a clear hill where the ascent to the top begins on the southwest face. The ascent is marked with stone milestones. The ice and the amount of snow will determine the technical level of the ascent. First there is a rocky step to a wide and comfortable shoulder, where the final attack begins through a channel that leads to the access passage to the geodesic vertex through a kind of tunnel. The views from the top of the remaining section of the rope are breathtaking between large granite domes and giant speedboats, but it is actually easier than the previous section and almost all descent. The descent of the peak is along the east face, to a pass where the Path of Zapatero climbs from the left from Sotalvo.
Canchal between Pico Zapatero and Risco del Sol

The path runs along the slopes of the mountain range and descends to the spectacular pass of Portacho del Cuchillo, below the impressive mass of spurs, towers and channels of Peña Cabrera. At the pass you have to pass the gate of a livestock lane and go to the south slope. The path begins the descent directly through the head of the Cuchillo Ravine, passing at the foot of the striking Risco del Cuchillo boat, one of the most unique geological formations in the Zapatero massif.

faqs.question: Where does the Pico Zapatero Circular Trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The trail typically begins in the town of Navandrinal. You can find parking options within or near the town to access the trailhead.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: The Pico Zapatero Circular Trail is rated as moderate in difficulty. While it offers rewarding views, it involves a significant elevation gain and some steep sections, including a 'pretty steep descent' and navigating large rocks. It might be challenging for absolute beginners or very young children, but experienced hikers and active families could enjoy it.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Pico Zapatero Circular Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features varied terrain. You'll encounter ascents past points like Portacho del Cuchillo, navigate large rocks, and experience a 'small climb' to the summit of Pico Zapatero, followed by a 'pretty steep descent' on one side. The path often runs parallel to the Zapatero Gorge, with zigzags and deviations.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights and viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers spectacular panoramic views, especially from the summit of Pico Zapatero. From there, you can see the Sierra de Guadarrama and Gredos. Other notable points include Cancha Morena (2,122 m), Portacho del Cuchillo, and the distinctive crag known as Zapaterillo, which is adorned with a cross.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Pico Zapatero Circular Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed in different seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be hot, so an early start is recommended. Winter hiking is possible but requires appropriate gear and experience due to potential snow and ice at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Pico Zapatero Circular Trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally welcome on the trail. However, it's advisable to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife, and to ensure you carry out any waste.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or is there an entrance fee for the trail?
faqs.answer: The Pico Zapatero Circular Trail is located within the Sierras de la Paramera y La Serrota Natural Space. Generally, there are no specific permits required for hiking this trail, nor are there entrance fees. However, always respect local regulations and environmental guidelines.

faqs.question: Are there any public transport options to reach the trailhead in Navandrinal?
faqs.answer: Public transport options to Navandrinal can be limited. It's recommended to check local bus schedules from larger towns like Ávila or San Juan del Molinillo, as direct routes might not be frequent. Driving is often the most convenient way to reach the trailhead.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's moderate difficulty and varied terrain, we recommend bringing sturdy hiking boots, sufficient water, snacks, sun protection, and layers of clothing suitable for changing mountain weather. A map or GPS device is also advisable.

faqs.question: Are there any places to eat or stay near the Pico Zapatero Circular Trail?
faqs.answer: The starting point, Navandrinal, is a small town, but you may find some local accommodation or dining options. For a wider selection of restaurants, cafes, pubs, and accommodation, consider nearby towns like San Juan del Molinillo or larger centers in the Ávila province.

faqs.question: What is the highest point on the trail?
faqs.answer: The highest point you will reach on this circular trail is the summit of Pico Zapatero, which stands at an elevation of 2,158 meters (7,080 feet). It is the highest peak in the Sierra de la Paramera.
